title: Traffic Lights & Robots
description: An engaging, hands-on introduction to how traffic lights work and how computers make decisions — using games, visuals, and guided play.
outcomes: Learn why traffic lights exist and how they help communities
Identify examples of technology that helps people
Complete a simple sorting activity that mimics AI logic

agenda.activity: Story: How does a traffic light know when to change?
Live demo: Watch the traffic simulation

title: Sensors, Data & Smart Signals
description: Students explore how sensor data powers smart traffic signals, run the simulation in two modes, and discuss the tradeoffs between fixed-timer and sensor-based systems.
outcomes: Explain how induction-loop sensors detect vehicles
Compare fixed-timer vs. actuated (sensor) traffic control
Read and interpret simulation metrics (wait time, throughput)
Discuss real-world tradeoffs in smart city technology

agenda.activity: Mini-lecture: Sensors, IoT, and smart infrastructure
Guided simulation: Run Mode A and Mode B, record metrics
Group discussion: Which mode performed better and why?

title: AI, Reinforcement Learning & Smart Cities
description: A deep dive into AI-powered adaptive traffic control using Reinforcement Learning concepts, data analysis, and cybersecurity considerations for smart infrastructure.
outcomes: Understand Reinforcement Learning: agents, states, rewards
Run all three simulation modes and analyze performance data
Identify cybersecurity vulnerabilities in smart city systems
Connect STEM skills to AI engineering career pathways

agenda.activity: Lecture + demo: RL Q-Learning applied to traffic signals
Simulation lab: All three modes, data collection & comparison
Cybersecurity module: Smart city attack surfaces
